free_text: Level densities for 69,71Ga nuclei using a
particle-evaporation technique
74,76Ge level densities.
Canon Medical Research Inc., Vernon Hills,
Illinois 60061, USA
The Edwards Laboratory tandem
electrostatic accelerator.
The dE-E telescope consisting of a thin
(0.25 mm Si) and a thick [5 mm Si(Li)] detectors
was consecutively placed at the 37, 52, 97, and 142
degree angles in respect to the beam direction inside
the chamber to register high-energy protons (from 7
up to 25 MeV).
Proton spectra and double differential cross
sections were measured from 7Li-induced reactions on
63,65Cu with a 15.5 MeV lithium beam. Protons were
measured at four angles: 37, 52, 97, and 142 degrees
in the laboratory frame.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
Approved by A.V.Voinov on October 15, 2025.
BP
2 mg/cm2 thick cooper self-
supporting foils enriched to 95% 63Cu.

Fig. 2b
Data were provided by A.V.Voinov on October 13, 2025.
The spectra measured at 97 and 142 degrees were used
to extract level densities for 69 (1-st set) and 71
(second sect) Ga nuclei. The experimental level
density excitation energy dependence rho(E*)exp is
obtained from proton spectra dY(ep)) /dep using the
following formula: rho(E*)exp = rho(E*)calc *
Knorm*(dY(ep))/dep)exp/(dY(ep))/dep)calc, where Knorm
is a normalization coefficient calculated as a ratio of
spectra integrals in the high-energy region between
E1 and E2, where primary protons populate discrete
levels of product nuclei.
Fig. 3
Data were provided by A.V.Voinov on October 13, 2025.
